name: Delete basket (clear after checkout)

targetRestContract.notes: Called as the final step of CheckoutModel.OnPost after the order is successfully created — the basket is purged once the order exists. CheckoutModel is [Authorize], meaning this is always called for authenticated users in legacy; the REST target should require JWT auth. Guard.Against.Null throws if the basket is not found — the REST target should return 404 instead.

notes: Deletes the entire Basket row and all its BasketItems rows (cascade delete via EF Core).
Called post-order-creation — not a user-initiated standalone delete; it is the cleanup step of the checkout flow.
Guard.Against.Null(basket) throws ArgumentNullException if the basket row does not exist. In legacy this surfaces as HTTP 500. The REST target should return 404.
The service accepts basketId (integer PK), not buyerId — the basket must already exist.
